title: Profile views allow anonymous access and crash on edit
reasoning: CustomUserView and CustomUserEditView read and mutate request.user without LoginRequiredMixin or an explicit authentication check. An anonymous GET to the edit view builds CustomUserForm(instance=request.user), where request.user is AnonymousUser rather than CustomUser, which can raise when the ModelForm tries to read model fields. The POST path has the same issue and reaches a profile update form without enforcing authentication. The password-change view in the same file does use LoginRequiredMixin, showing protected account pages are expected to require login.
reproduction: Request /customuser/edit/ while logged out. The view enters CustomUserEditView.get and constructs CustomUserForm(instance=request.user) with AnonymousUser.
recommendation: Make CustomUserView and CustomUserEditView inherit LoginRequiredMixin before View, or otherwise redirect unauthenticated users before constructing forms or querying profile data.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: Users/tests.py contains only the default stub and no anonymous-access tests for profile routes.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add tests asserting logged-out requests to users:customuser and users:edit_user redirect to the login page, and logged-in users can view and update only their own profile.
minimumFixScope: Update CustomUserView and CustomUserEditView inheritance or dispatch handling, plus route-level tests.
